scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal manumission has a direct material effect on status and economic freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Manumission is a broad category of legal transition from property to personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Manumission is a lifelong structural change in status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural legal birth-status, very hard to change without manumission.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aeschines refers to a statute in force in contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites an express legal prohibition ('He expressly forbids...') as a factual datum.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The prohibition of theater-specific manumission is not inherent to the definition of a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A golden crown is tangible wealth/property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the handling of foreign honorary gifts.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The loss of the physical property (dedication) is permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Athenian law regarding rewards.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Quotes the law's command and intention as a factual matter.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually imply the inability to keep foreign gifts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to address the assembly has direct material effects on legislation and policy.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political speech in the sovereign assembly is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right to speak in the assembly is a lifelong privilege of citizenship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the active political life of contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Narrates the exercise of a standard, attested civic right.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of citizen does not always include active rhetorical rights in all societies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting on ambassadors controls foreign policy, affecting war, peace, and trade.
scores.generality_reasoning: The power to elect representatives is a broad political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right to vote on state motions is a lifelong structural right.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Athenian assembly procedures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes actual legislative motions and democratic procedures as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Choosing ambassadors is a specific procedural right, not a definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting on decrees determines the legal and material conditions of the state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Legislative voting is a foundational societal axis of power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Participation in popular sovereignty is a lifelong citizen right.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship in Oreus is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the contemporary democratic government of a neighboring city.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Aeschines provides the decree as proof of the factual voting procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The right to vote on decrees is a specific mechanism of democratic citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land cultivation is a primary means of subsistence and wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Ban on land use spans all agricultural and economic activity for the area.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The ban is intended to be perpetual ('for the future it lie entirely uncultivated').
scores.immutability_reasoning: 'All people' is a universal category based on presence, not an immutable group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a historical oracle and oath from the First Sacred War.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the religious decree as a matter of historical and traditional fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The group 'everyone' does not define land-use rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Holding high office grants control over state resources and law.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for the archonship is a major political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility for office is a lifelong structural feature of citizen status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary election of officials in recent years.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Lists specific individuals and their election as plain historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Office eligibility is an attribute of citizenship, not its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting in the Amphictyonic Council has international legal consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to representation in a particular international body.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tied to the term of office, but reflects long-term state representational status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Hieromnemons are high-status citizens chosen through structural processes.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary rules governing the council's operation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explains the technical constitutional procedure of the council.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Representational voting is a specific procedural rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Compulsory physical labor involves the body and means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific legal obligation to provide labor for a particular project.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Describes a specific emergency call ('on the morrow').
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by sex, age, and location, which are immutable in context.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes an event occurring in the author's own political sphere.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Quotes a herald's proclamation and public decree matter-of-factly.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a man in Delphi does not inherently mean being a laborer for the Thyteion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participation in a deliberative assembly has social and political impact.
scores.generality_reasoning: Limited to attendance at a specific religious-political assembly.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Right is bounded to the specific occasion of sacrifice or consultation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined by a transient behavioral event (sacrificing or consulting).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Explains the contemporary definitions of council terminology.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Provides a factual definition of what the assembly consists of.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Performing a sacrifice is not defined by assembly participation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Citizenship determines legal personhood and access to all state resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational requirement for all other rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status is determined by birth and remains lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined by parentage and blood (lineage), strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary Athenian peripatetic/citizenship law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: While used in an attack, the underlying parentage law is a reported fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A group with specific parentage is not tautologically synonymous with legal citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to practice a trade (ferryman) is a direct source of wealth/subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific professional license.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of the right is permanent ('permits him no longer').
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation is a durable social identity acquired through the profession.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Quotes a law passed by contemporary Athenian citizens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific statute and the condition of its enforcement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a ferryman does not define the right to remain one after an accident.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Provides food, support, and expensive military equipment (armor).
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a social welfare program for a particular lineage group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bouned to the life stage of childhood/coming of age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on the immutable fact of a father's death in war.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes an ongoing and traditional Athenian public custom.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes an established and observable public practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a war orphan does not conceptually define receipt of armor.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Seats of honor are symbolic ceremonial honors.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to theater seating protocol.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Linked to the period of their public recognition/coming of age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on the immutable fact of a father's death in war.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common public honors in contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Observes a factual proclamation and public procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Sons of fallen soldiers are not defined by theater seating.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political leadership controls state policy and resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for leadership is a broad political category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Based on hereditary factors which do not change over a lifetime.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by lineage and birth status (free-born, double parentage).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary expectations for Athenian politicians.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author explicitly frames this as an opinion ('I think you would all acknowledge').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Friendship to the people is not defined by free birth.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the Agora (the economic and legal center) is essential for civic life.
scores.generality_reasoning: The Agora is a foundational setting for various life activities.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of these rights for military failure is a permanent civic penalty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by behavior (cowardice) that leads to a durable legal status identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Laws of Solon were active statutes in contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the lawgiver's exclusion directly as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Cowardice is not defined as exclusion from the Agora.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Crowns and honors are legal resources with status and material impact.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for public honors is a broad civic category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Stigma and legal exclusion from honors are permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on a military action that creates a lasting legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary laws governing honors.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites a legal exclusion as a factual constraint.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Cowardice is not defined by the lack of a crown.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Religious participation has significant social and legal outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers all 'sacred rites of the people,' a broad class of activity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion is a permanent change in civic religious status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on military conduct that creates a durable legal category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to active statutes regarding religious purity and conduct.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a factual legal exclusion by the lawgiver.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A soldier who deserts is not defined by religious exclusion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Theater access is a key civic and social resource in Athens.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to access to theater and particular shrines.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of right is part of a structural lifelong civic penalty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status is based on specific past behavior (betrayal/cowardice).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary legal restrictions in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Argues that a fact (loss of right) is being violated by a decree.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Cowardice does not conceptually mean theater-exclusion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Crowns have legal value and signal status; the review is a material hurdle.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the awarding of olive crowns for particular services.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The award/honor is a permanent mark of status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on a specific historical military action (siege at Phyle).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary senate review procedures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a factual administrative procedure of the Senate.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Standing siege is successfully distinct from receiving a crown.
